title: Senate Hall
content:

The '''Senate Hall''', '''Republic Senate Chamber''', '''Senate agora''', or '''Old Senate Chamber''' was the building that housed the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] in [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] from the earliest [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Old Sith Wars]]. Eventually, it was replaced by a bigger, less intimate structure known as the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]].

==History==
{{Quote|Baas went to Coruscant, to speak to his disciple, to get him to return to the way of the Jedi. Kun killed him in the Senate Chamber.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]]|<ref name="I, Jedi">''[[I, Jedi]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Galactic_Senate_3996BBY.jpg|thumb|250px|left|Senate Hall in [[3996 BBY]]]]
A large stone structure on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the Senate Hall was used to convene sessions of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] during the institution's early millennia of existence. The [[Senate Hall guards|Republic Senate Guardsmen]] were responsible for guarding not only the building, but the [[Senator/Legends|senators]], [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], [[Vice Chair/Legends|Vice Chair]], and the [[Senior Administrative Aide/Legends|Staff Aide]] during senatorial meetings. The building was also used during formal trials.<ref name="TOTJTSW3" />

In [[3996 BBY]], during the [[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma|trial]] of the fallen [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] entered the Hall, placing the assembled senators and [[Sidrona|the Supreme Chancellor]] under his control as he stormed the chamber. Following his execution of the Chancellor, Kun [[Duel in the Senate|engaged]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] in a duel, killing his former master before freeing Qel-Droma.<ref name="TOTJTSW3" />

[[File:TrialOfDemagol.jpg|thumb|300px|right|The trial of Demagol was held in the Senate Hall]]
Following the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], the Senate commissioned the construction of a new Senate Hall to accommodate the increasing number of member worlds within the Republic. On the foundations of the old Senate Hall, the new [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Tower]] was established, crowned by a massive rotunda that rested over a hundred levels above the old hall. Coming into disuse, the old Senate Hall was used for the [[Trial of Demagol|trial]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Demagol]] in [[3963 BBY]] for his role in the early [[Mandalorian Wars]].<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 47">[[Knights of the Old Republic 47|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 47]]</ref> [[3962 BBY|One year later]], the agora was used for [[Trial of Dorjander Kace|another show trial]], that of the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Dorjander Kace]] and his followers, the [[Mandalorian Knights]].<ref name="KotOR: War 5">[[Knights of the Old Republic: War 5|''Knights of the Old Republic: War'' 5]]</ref> At some point in its history, the Hall was retired and became a tourist attraction for the throngs of civilians who visited Coruscant daily.<ref name="DP">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref>

==Layout==
[[File:SenateHall-TGAotS4.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The entrance of the Senate Hall]]
A massive stone and glass building, the Senate Hall was located at the heart of the [[Senate District/Legends|Senate District]] on Coruscant. Originally an open-air stadium, the Senate Hall was enclosed within a towering A-frame structure with walls sloping up and inward, following the attack on Coruscant by the Sith. The facility had a glass facade which allowed natural light to spill into the facility, and was located next to a large spaceport. The main entrance to the building was positioned at the top of a massive staircase and past an ornately decorated doorway. Beyond the entrance was a lofty chamber which made up the heart of the building, featuring stadium-style seating with stone benches accommodating a very large number of senators. The center of the massive room was occupied by the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor's]] podium, a massive stone column ringed by a spiraling staircase leading to the smooth pinnacle platform. A smaller podium rose from the floor in front of the Chancellor's; this platform was used by delegations who wished to formally address the Senate, as well as an area used to hold criminals during formal trials.<ref name="TOTJTSW3" />

The upper level of the room was open to foot traffic and from which massive pillars stretched to the ceiling. At either end of the hall, dual statues of massive winged [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoids]] knelt inward towards a large flaming podium.<ref name="TOTJTSW3" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Senate_HSW.jpg|thumb|right|275px|The Grand Convocation Chamber, nearly a millennium before its date of construction during the Great Hyperspace War.]]
Counter to all prior sources stating that the [[Grand Convocation Chamber]] was erected following the [[Duel in the Senate|duel in the old Senate Hall]] between [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], ''[[Timeline 12: The Great Hyperspace War]]'' depicts the Chamber in operation nearly one thousand years prior to when it should first make an appearance. [[Ludo Kressh]]'s appearance in the same ''Timeline'' video is also inaccurate to his appearance as shown in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']], and the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] spotted in the video are inaccurately portrayed too. Such discrepancies could be explained away as artistic license.

There is a small inconsistency of the interior of the Senate Hall between [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3]] and [[Knights of the Old Republic 47]]. In Knights of the Old Republic, additional pillars can be seen between the winged humanoid statues and the senate chamber, however in Tales of the Jedi these pillars seem to be absent.
